{{Infobox ancient site |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Tumulus Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: * Diameter: ~53.7 meters * Height: ~8 meters * Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: * Width: ~33.5 meters * Height: ~4 meters * Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: * Incised flower pattern mirror (1) * Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) * Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: * Jade magatama (4) * Jasper cylindrical beads * Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: * Iron swords (6 fragments) * Iron axes (3) * Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: * Jasper wheel-shaped stone * Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: * Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} (Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |Artifacts = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}
