revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |Latitude = 32.6632 |Longitude = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |Size = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} revision.comment: another mass edit to try to fix parameters

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} The tumulus is keyhole-typed, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-typed stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-typed stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-typed stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-typed tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-typed stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} (Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as Important other_designation of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as Important other_designation of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as Important other_designation of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == Artifacts == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]] Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun Artifacts}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designated historic site; grave goods designated as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated artifacts are recognized as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designated as a city historic site, and in 1979, the artifacts were designated as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These artifacts are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designated on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays artifacts from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]] (Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-designation1 historic site; grave goods designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is designation1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was designation1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - designation1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - designation1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]] revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-other_designation1 historic site; grave goods other_designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was other_designation1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were other_designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mukunoda Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mukunoda Kofun}}[[Category:11]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-other_designation1 historic site; grave goods other_designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was other_designation1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were other_designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mukunoda Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mukunoda Kofun}}[[Category:11]] Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was other_designation1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were other_designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |Era = Late 4th century |Burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-other_designation1 historic site; grave goods other_designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was other_designation1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were other_designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == other_designation == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City Designation === * other_designation: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - other_designation1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City other_designations}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References ==
