Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]] {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Important designation1 (Japan)]] {{Kofun navbar}}

The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} of Japan]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft|12=draft}} [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]

revision.comment: 16 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:向野田古墳 up to rev 99995841
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 向野田古墳 |画像 = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
後円部墳頂
（中央に墓壙1段目。2段目は埋没） |所属 = |所在地 = [[熊本県]][[宇土市]]松山町 |緯度度 = 32|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 47.52 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 40|経度秒 = 25.53 |ISO = JP-43 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長86m
高さ8m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]1基
（内部に[[舟形石棺]]1基） |被葬者 = 成人女性1人 |出土品 = 人骨・副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |史跡 = 宇土市指定史跡「向野田古墳」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kumamoto |ラベル = 向野田古墳 |ラベル位置 = top |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北 }} '''向野田古墳'''（むこうのだこふん）は、[[熊本県]][[宇土市]]松山町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。宇土市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 熊本県中部、[[熊本平野]]・[[八代平野]]の間の丘陵上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。これまでに採土工事による大きな改変を受けているほか、[[1967年|1967]]-[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]42-44年）および[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北方に向ける。墳丘は3段築成と見られるが、採土工事で前方部が削平されているほか、後円部墳頂も改変を受けている。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]のほか[[葺石]]が認められる。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に[[舟形石棺]]を据える{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。石棺内外からは被葬者の人骨（成人女性）のほか、銅鏡・玉類・車輪石・刀剣類・工具類など多数の副葬品が検出されている。以上より築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。人骨の出土のほか、被葬者が女性単独という点で注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。 古墳域の後円部部分は[[1969年]]（昭和44年）に宇土市指定史跡に指定されたほか、出土品は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1967年|1967]]-[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]42-44年）、発掘調査（宇土市教育委員会、[[1978年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。 * 1969年（昭和44年）10月1日、宇土市指定史跡に指定。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）6月6日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、範囲確認の墳丘発掘調査（宇土市教育委員会）{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り宇土市立図書館郷土資料室 展示解説。。 * 墳丘長：約86メートル - 他文献では97メートル{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 後円部 - 3段築成か。現在は上面は削平。 ** 直径：約53.7メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成か。現在は消滅。 ** 幅：約33.5メートル ** 高さ：約4メートル 元の墳丘は宇土市内では3番目、熊本県内では7番目の規模であった。 == 埋葬施設 == [[ファイル:向野田古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|宇土市立図書館郷土資料室展示。}}]] 埋葬施設は後円部における[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に[[舟形石棺]]を据える{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室は、後円部中央の内法長4.25メートル・北側幅1.1メートル・南側幅0.94メートル・高さ1.08メートルの墓壙の中央において、割石小口積みで形成される{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石棺は阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製の刳抜式舟形石棺で、両端には縄掛突起を有する{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。寸法は、棺身内法長2.86メートル・北側幅0.52メートル・南側幅0.45メートル・深さ0.33メートル{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石棺内からは北を頭位とする人骨（30代後半-40代の成人女性）のほか銅鏡・玉類・車輪石などの副葬品が、石棺・石室の間からは刀剣類・工具類などの副葬品が検出された{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。これらの副葬品は国の重要文化財に指定され、現在は宇土市立図書館の郷土資料室に保管・展示されている。 ファイル:向野田古墳出土 銅鏡・玉類・刀剣類.JPG|銅鏡・玉類・刀剣類 ファイル:向野田古墳出土 南海製イモ貝製貝釧.JPG|南海製イモ貝製貝釧 ファイル:向野田古墳出土 石枕未成品.JPG|石枕未成品
{{small|石室の裏ごめ石に混入して出土。}} ファイル:向野田古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形円筒埴輪片
{{small|突帯が幅広い特徴を有する。}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 肥後向野田古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 宇土市立図書館郷土資料室保管。明細は以下。1979年（昭和54年）6月6日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9990|肥後向野田古墳出土品}}[http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohunsyutudohin.html 向野田古墳出土品]（宇土市「宇土市デジタルミュージアム」）。。 ** 銅鏡 *** 内行花文鏡 1面 *** [[方格規矩鏡]] 1面 *** 禽獣鏡 1面 ** 玉類 *** 硬玉勾玉 4箇 *** 碧玉管玉 一括 *** ガラス小玉 残欠共 一括 ** 碧玉車輪石 1箇 ** 刀剣類 *** 刀身 残欠共 6口 *** 剣身 4口 ** 工具類 *** 鉄斧 3箇 *** 刀子 残欠共 78口 === 宇土市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 向野田古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）10月1日指定[http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohun.html 向野田古墳]（宇土市「宇土市デジタルミュージアム」）。。 == 関連施設 == * 宇土市立図書館 郷土資料室（宇土市浦田町） - 向野田古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（宇土市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=向野田古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 44 熊本県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490441|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=向野田古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=木崎康弘|year=2002|chapter=向野田古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=島津義昭|year=2006|chapter=向野田古墳|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=[[大和書房]]|isbn=978-4479840657|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（古代史）|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99355 向野田古墳（宇土市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第2集）]|publisher=宇土市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9990|肥後向野田古墳出土品}} * [http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohun.html 向野田古墳]、[http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohunsyutudohin.html 向野田古墳出土品] - 宇土市「宇土市デジタルミュージアム」 {{デフォルトソート:むこうのたこふん}} [[Category:熊本県の古墳]] [[Category:宇土市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:熊本県の重要文化財]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[向野田古墳]] to [[Mukunoda Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 向野田古墳 |画像 = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
後円部墳頂
（中央に墓壙1段目。2段目は埋没） |所属 = |所在地 = [[熊本県]][[宇土市]]松山町 |緯度度 = 32|緯度分 = 39|緯度秒 = 47.52 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 40|経度秒 = 25.53 |ISO = JP-43 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長86m
高さ8m（後円部） |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]1基
（内部に[[舟形石棺]]1基） |被葬者 = 成人女性1人 |出土品 = 人骨・副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |史跡 = 宇土市指定史跡「向野田古墳」 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kumamoto |ラベル = 向野田古墳 |ラベル位置 = top |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北 }} '''向野田古墳'''（むこうのだこふん）は、[[熊本県]][[宇土市]]松山町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。宇土市指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 熊本県中部、[[熊本平野]]・[[八代平野]]の間の丘陵上に築造された古墳である{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。これまでに採土工事による大きな改変を受けているほか、[[1967年|1967]]-[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]42-44年）および[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北方に向ける。墳丘は3段築成と見られるが、採土工事で前方部が削平されているほか、後円部墳頂も改変を受けている。墳丘表面では[[円筒埴輪]]のほか[[葺石]]が認められる。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に[[舟形石棺]]を据える{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。石棺内外からは被葬者の人骨（成人女性）のほか、銅鏡・玉類・車輪石・刀剣類・工具類など多数の副葬品が検出されている。以上より築造時期は[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。人骨の出土のほか、被葬者が女性単独という点で注目される古墳になる{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。 古墳域の後円部部分は[[1969年]]（昭和44年）に宇土市指定史跡に指定されたほか、出土品は[[1979年]]（昭和54年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1967年|1967]]-[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]42-44年）、発掘調査（宇土市教育委員会、[[1978年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。 * 1969年（昭和44年）10月1日、宇土市指定史跡に指定。 * [[1979年]]（昭和54年）6月6日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、範囲確認の墳丘発掘調査（宇土市教育委員会）{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り宇土市立図書館郷土資料室 展示解説。。 * 墳丘長：約86メートル - 他文献では97メートル{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 後円部 - 3段築成か。現在は上面は削平。 ** 直径：約53.7メートル ** 高さ：約8メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成か。現在は消滅。 ** 幅：約33.5メートル ** 高さ：約4メートル 元の墳丘は宇土市内では3番目、熊本県内では7番目の規模であった。 == 埋葬施設 == [[ファイル:向野田古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|宇土市立図書館郷土資料室展示。}}]] 埋葬施設は後円部における[[竪穴式石室]]で、内部に[[舟形石棺]]を据える{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室は、後円部中央の内法長4.25メートル・北側幅1.1メートル・南側幅0.94メートル・高さ1.08メートルの墓壙の中央において、割石小口積みで形成される{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 石棺は阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製の刳抜式舟形石棺で、両端には縄掛突起を有する{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。寸法は、棺身内法長2.86メートル・北側幅0.52メートル・南側幅0.45メートル・深さ0.33メートル{{Sfn|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石棺内からは北を頭位とする人骨（30代後半-40代の成人女性）のほか銅鏡・玉類・車輪石などの副葬品が、石棺・石室の間からは刀剣類・工具類などの副葬品が検出された{{Sfn|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}。これらの副葬品は国の重要文化財に指定され、現在は宇土市立図書館の郷土資料室に保管・展示されている。 ファイル:向野田古墳出土 銅鏡・玉類・刀剣類.JPG|銅鏡・玉類・刀剣類 ファイル:向野田古墳出土 南海製イモ貝製貝釧.JPG|南海製イモ貝製貝釧 ファイル:向野田古墳出土 石枕未成品.JPG|石枕未成品
{{small|石室の裏ごめ石に混入して出土。}} ファイル:向野田古墳出土 円筒埴輪・朝顔形円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪・朝顔形円筒埴輪片
{{small|突帯が幅広い特徴を有する。}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 肥後向野田古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 宇土市立図書館郷土資料室保管。明細は以下。1979年（昭和54年）6月6日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9990|肥後向野田古墳出土品}}[http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohunsyutudohin.html 向野田古墳出土品]（宇土市「宇土市デジタルミュージアム」）。。 ** 銅鏡 *** 内行花文鏡 1面 *** [[方格規矩鏡]] 1面 *** 禽獣鏡 1面 ** 玉類 *** 硬玉勾玉 4箇 *** 碧玉管玉 一括 *** ガラス小玉 残欠共 一括 ** 碧玉車輪石 1箇 ** 刀剣類 *** 刀身 残欠共 6口 *** 剣身 4口 ** 工具類 *** 鉄斧 3箇 *** 刀子 残欠共 78口 === 宇土市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 向野田古墳 - 1969年（昭和44年）10月1日指定[http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohun.html 向野田古墳]（宇土市「宇土市デジタルミュージアム」）。。 == 関連施設 == * 宇土市立図書館 郷土資料室（宇土市浦田町） - 向野田古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（宇土市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=向野田古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 44 熊本県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490441|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（平凡社）|1985}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林三郎|authorlink=小林三郎 (考古学者)|year=1989|chapter=向野田古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=木崎康弘|year=2002|chapter=向野田古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=島津義昭|year=2006|chapter=向野田古墳|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=[[大和書房]]|isbn=978-4479840657|ref={{Harvid|向野田古墳（古代史）|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/99355 向野田古墳（宇土市埋蔵文化財調査報告書 第2集）]|publisher=宇土市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9990|肥後向野田古墳出土品}} * [http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohun.html 向野田古墳]、[http://www.city.uto.kumamoto.jp/museum/pro/kodai/mukounodakohunsyutudohin.html 向野田古墳出土品] - 宇土市「宇土市デジタルミュージアム」 {{デフォルトソート:むこうのたこふん}} [[Category:熊本県の古墳]] [[Category:宇土市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:熊本県の重要文化財]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]
Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan}} [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = 32.6632 |coordinates = 130.6738 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|ja|宇土市|12=draft}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped stone coffin|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sekkan|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Mukunoda Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:floating "of Japan"]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[en:Mukunoda Kofun]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[sn:ukunoda Kofun]]
