Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto, Kumamoto]], Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|32.6632|N|130.673758|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto, Kumamoto]], Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. Second layer buried) |location = [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto, Kumamoto]], Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|32.6632|N|130.673758|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto, Kumamoto]], Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in [[:en:Uto, Kumamoto|Uto, Kumamoto]], Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:向野田古墳]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28688915]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Mukōnoda Kofun |image = [[File:Mukonoda Kofun, funcho.jpg|280px]]

In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Top of the rear mound
(Central burial chamber first layer. Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto, Kumamoto}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|32.6632|N|130.673758|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} of Japan |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto, Kumamoto}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto, Kumamoto|1=Uto, Kumamoto}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|32.6632|N|130.673758|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|WD=Q861262|lt=Uto, Kumamoto|1=Uto, Kumamoto}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Second layer buried) |location = {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|qid=Q861262|lt=Uto, Kumamoto|1=Uto, Kumamoto}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|32.6632|N|130.673758|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length: 86 m
Height: 8 m (rear mound) |built = Late 4th century |burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} |burial goods = Human remains, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Cultural property = City-Historic Site1 historic site; grave goods Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Mukōnoda Kofun''' (向野田古墳) is a keyhole-shaped tumulus located in {{ill|Uto, Kumamoto|en|Uto, Kumamoto|qid=Q861262|lt=Uto, Kumamoto|1=Uto, Kumamoto}}, Matsuyama Town, Kumamoto Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by the city of Uto, and its excavated burial goods are recognized as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan. == Overview == Mukōnoda Kofun is situated on a hill between the Kumamoto and Yatsushiro Plains. The tumulus has undergone significant modifications due to soil extraction and has been subject to excavations in 1967–1969 and 1992.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with its front section oriented northward. The mound, originally constructed in three tiers, has suffered extensive damage, including leveling of the front section and alterations to the top of the rear mound.{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, housing a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}}. Human remains of an adult female, aged 30–40 years, along with numerous grave goods including bronze mirrors, beads, swords, and tools, were recovered.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. The tumulus dates to the late 4th century.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. In 1969, the rear mound area was Historic Site1 as a city historic site, and in 1979, the burial goods were Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan.. == Kofun Details == The mound’s area are as follows:{{Sfn|Exhibit at Uto City Library}}. * Total length: 86 meters (other references state 97 meters) * Rear mound: ** Diameter: ~53.7 meters ** Height: ~8 meters ** Constructed in three tiers * Front mound: ** Width: ~33.5 meters ** Height: ~4 meters ** Constructed in three tiers; currently destroyed Originally, it was the third-largest tumulus in Uto and the seventh-largest in Kumamoto Prefecture. == burial == The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located at the center of the rear mound. It is constructed using stacked stones and houses a {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun (Heibonsha)|1985}}. The coffin features rope-pattern protrusions on both ends and measures: * Coffin interior length: 2.86 meters * Interior width: 0.52 meters (north) to 0.45 meters (south) * Interior depth: 0.33 meters Human remains (head positioned north) and grave goods were found both inside and outside the coffin, including bronze mirrors, beads, and tools. == burial goods == Notable grave goods include: * Bronze mirrors: ** Incised flower pattern mirror (1) ** Rectangular-gridded mirror (1) ** Animal design mirror (1) * Beads: ** Jade magatama (4) ** Jasper cylindrical beads ** Glass beads (fragments included) * Tools and weapons: ** Iron swords (6 fragments) ** Iron axes (3) ** Small knives (78 fragments) * Other items: ** Jasper wheel-shaped stone ** Cylindrical haniwa and fragments These burial goods are displayed at the Uto City Library Local History Room.. == Historic Site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on June 6, 1979. Now housed and displayed in the Uto City Library.{{Sfn|Mukōnoda Kofun burial goods}}. === Uto City designation1 === * Historic Site: ** Mukōnoda Kofun - Historic Site1 on October 1, 1969.{{Sfn|Uto City Historic Sites}}. == Related Facilities == * Uto City Library Local History Room (Uto City, Urata Town) - houses and displays burial goods from Mukōnoda Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺|1=Funagata Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boat-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagata Sarcophagus|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|向野田古墳|version=99995841|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:向野田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28688915}} [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kumamoto prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Uto, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpōkōenfun]]
